OBSIDIAN_HOST.description: Hostname or IP address where the Obsidian REST API plugin is running

OBSIDIAN_PORT.description: Port number for the Obsidian REST API plugin

OBSIDIAN_API_KEY.description: API key for the Obsidian REST API plugin

deploymentOptions.description: Run the server using the 'uvx mcp-obsidian' command. Requires environment variables OBSIDIAN_API_KEY (mandatory), OBSIDIAN_HOST (default 127.0.0.1), and OBSIDIAN_PORT (default 27124).

installInstructions.manual: Install Python 3.11 or higher from https://www.python.org/downloads/

OBSIDIAN_HOST.description: Hostname or IP address where the Obsidian REST API plugin is running

OBSIDIAN_PORT.description: Port number for the Obsidian REST API plugin

OBSIDIAN_API_KEY.description: API key for the Obsidian REST API plugin

deploymentOptions.description: Run the server from source by cloning the repo and running 'uv run mcp-obsidian'. Replace '<dir_to>/mcp-obsidian' with the actual local path to the cloned repository. Requires environment variables OBSIDIAN_API_KEY (mandatory), OBSIDIAN_HOST (default 127.0.0.1), and OBSIDIAN_PORT (default 27124).

setupSteps: Install dependencies and sync lockfile using 'uv sync'
Run the server with 'uv run mcp-obsidian' in the repository directory

installInstructions.manual: Install Python 3.11 or higher from https://www.python.org/downloads/

description: 用于与 Obsidian 通过本地 REST API 社区插件交互的 MCP 服务器。需要预先安装并运行 Obsidian REST API 社区插件。通过环境变量或服务器配置，使用 Obsidian API 密钥、主机和端口来配置服务器。
name: Obsidian 的 MCP 服务器

overview.readme:
# MCP server for Obsidian MCP server to interact with Obsidian via the Local REST API community plugin. ## Components ### Tools The server implements multiple tools to interact with Obsidian: - list_files_in_vault: Lists all files and directories in the root directory of your Obsidian vault - list_files_in_dir: Lists all files and directories in a specific Obsidian directory - get_file_contents: Return the content of a single file in your vault. - search: Search for documents matching a specified text query across all files in the vault - patch_content: Insert content into an existing note relative to a heading, block reference, or frontmatter field. - append_content: Append content to a new or existing file in the vault. - delete_file: Delete a file or directory from your vault. ### Example prompts Its good to first instruct Claude to use Obsidian. Then it will always call the tool. The use prompts like this: - Get the contents of the last architecture call note and summarize them - Search for all files where Azure CosmosDb is mentioned and quickly explain to me the context in which it is mentioned - Summarize the last meeting notes and put them into a new note 'summary meeting.md'. Add an introduction so that I can send it via email. ## Configuration ### Obsidian REST API Key There are two ways to configure the environment with the Obsidian REST API Key. 1. Add to server config (preferred) ```json { "mcp-obsidian": { "command": "uvx", "args": [ "mcp-obsidian" ], "env": { "OBSIDIAN_API_KEY": "", "OBSIDIAN_HOST": "", "OBSIDIAN_PORT": "" } } } ``` Sometimes Claude has issues detecting the location of uv / uvx. You can use `which uvx` to find and paste the full path in above config in such cases. 2. Create a `.env` file in the working directory with the following required variables: ``` OBSIDIAN_API_KEY=your_api_key_here OBSIDIAN_HOST=your_obsidian_host OBSIDIAN_PORT=your_obsidian_port ``` Note: - You can find the API key in the Obsidian plugin config - Default port is 27124 if not specified - Default host is 127.0.0.1 if not specified ## Quickstart ### Install #### Obsidian REST API You need the Obsidian REST API community plugin running: https://github.com/coddingtonbear/obsidian-local-rest-api Install and enable it in the settings and copy the api key. #### Claude Desktop On MacOS: `~/Library/Application\ Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` On Windows: `%APPDATA%/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
Development/Unpublished Servers Configuration ```json { "mcpServers": { "mcp-obsidian": { "command": "uv", "args": [ "--directory", "/mcp-obsidian", "run", "mcp-obsidian" ], "env": { "OBSIDIAN_API_KEY": "", "OBSIDIAN_HOST": "", "OBSIDIAN_PORT": "" } } } } ```
Published Servers Configuration ```json { "mcpServers": { "mcp-obsidian": { "command": "uvx", "args": [ "mcp-obsidian" ], "env": { "OBSIDIAN_API_KEY": "", "OBSIDIAN_HOST": "", "OBSIDIAN_PORT": "" } } } } ```
## Development ### Building To prepare the package for distribution: 1. Sync dependencies and update lockfile: ```bash uv sync ``` ### Debugging Since MCP servers run over stdio, debugging can be challenging. For the best debugging experience, we strongly recommend using the [MCP Inspector](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector). You can launch the MCP Inspector via [`npm`](https://docs.npmjs.com/downloading-and-installing-node-js-and-npm) with this command: ```bash npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector uv --directory /path/to/mcp-obsidian run mcp-obsidian ``` Upon launching, the Inspector will display a URL that you can access in your browser to begin debugging. You can also watch the server logs with this command: ```bash tail -n 20 -f ~/Library/Logs/Claude/mcp-server-mcp-obsidian.log ```

tools.name: obsidian_list_files_in_dir
tools.description: Lists all files and directories that exist in a specific Obsidian directory.

dirpath.description: Path to list files from (relative to your vault root). Note that empty directories will not be returned.

tools.name: obsidian_list_files_in_vault
tools.description: Lists all files and directories in the root directory of your Obsidian vault.

tools.name: obsidian_get_file_contents
tools.description: Return the content of a single file in your vault.

filepath.description: Path to the relevant file (relative to your vault root).

tools.name: obsidian_simple_search
tools.description:
Simple search for documents matching a specified text query across all files in the vault. 
            Use this tool when you want to do a simple text search

query.description: Text to a simple search for in the vault.

context_length.description: How much context to return around the matching string (default: 100)

tools.name: obsidian_patch_content
tools.description: Insert content into an existing note relative to a heading, block reference, or frontmatter field.

target.description: Target identifier (heading path, block reference, or frontmatter field)

filepath.description: Path to the file (relative to vault root)

operation.description: Operation to perform (append, prepend, or replace)

tools.name: obsidian_append_content
tools.description: Append content to a new or existing file in the vault.

content.description: Content to append to the file

filepath.description: Path to the file (relative to vault root)

tools.name: obsidian_delete_file
tools.description: Delete a file or directory from the vault.

confirm.description: Confirmation to delete the file (must be true)

filepath.description: Path to the file or directory to delete (relative to vault root)

tools.name: obsidian_complex_search
tools.description:
Complex search for documents using a JsonLogic query. 
           Supports standard JsonLogic operators plus 'glob' and 'regexp' for pattern matching. Results must be non-falsy.

           Use this tool when you want to do a complex search, e.g. for all documents with certain tags etc.

query.description: JsonLogic query object. Example: {"glob": ["*.md", {"var": "path"}]} matches all markdown files

tools.name: obsidian_batch_get_file_contents
tools.description: Return the contents of multiple files in your vault, concatenated with headers.

items.description: Path to a file (relative to your vault root)

filepaths.description: List of file paths to read

tools.name: obsidian_get_periodic_note
tools.description: Get current periodic note for the specified period.

period.description: The period type (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly)

tools.name: obsidian_get_recent_periodic_notes
tools.description: Get most recent periodic notes for the specified period type.

limit.description: Maximum number of notes to return (default: 5)

period.description: The period type (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly)

include_content.description: Whether to include note content (default: false)

tools.name: obsidian_get_recent_changes
tools.description: Get recently modified files in the vault.

days.description: Only include files modified within this many days (default: 90)

limit.description: Maximum number of files to return (default: 10)
